operating_state: Net sales increased 8% to $269.9 billion driven by 6% comparable sales growth, 5% higher shopping frequency, and 24 net new warehouse openings to 914 locations globally. Executive membership penetration reached 73.6% with membership fee increases in U.S. and Canada contributing 40% of membership income growth. E-commerce comparable sales grew 15% with digitally-enabled sales reaching 10% of total including travel services.
cost_structure: Gross margin percentage increased 20 basis points to 11.12% driven by core merchandise improvements in fresh foods and co-branded credit card program, partially offset by LIFO charge from higher merchandise costs. Starting wages increased to $20.00 minimum with average U.S. hourly rate reaching $32.00, contributing to SG&A expense increase of 11 basis points to 9.25%. Kirkland Signature private-label expansion continued generating higher margins.
material_events: Board approved 12% quarterly dividend increase to $1.30 per share and repurchased 943,000 shares under $4.0 billion authorization expiring January 2027. Gary Millerchip joined as CFO from Kroger in February 2024 with multiple executive transitions announced including Russ Miller retiring February 2026. Extended producer responsibility laws across multiple states require new policies and fee payments to governments.
macro_exposures: Foreign currency translation negatively impacted net sales by $1.9 billion and net income by $97 million with forward contracts outstanding hedging international inventory purchases. Gasoline price deflation reduced net sales by $2.3 billion while trade-related tariff actions affected merchandise costs. Tax rate changes across jurisdictions create earnings volatility with transfer pricing disputes potentially material.
forward_outlook: Management plans to open up to 35 new warehouses in fiscal 2026 with capital expenditure guidance of $6.0-6.5 billion financed through operating cash flow. Technology investments continue emphasizing digital capabilities, supply chain efficiency, and cybersecurity infrastructure while Kirkland Signature expansion expected to increase sales penetration. Public sustainability targets committed requiring future capital expenditures for emissions standards.
uncertainty_profile: Cybersecurity threats increasing in frequency with potential for operational disruption and regulatory penalties under evolving global privacy regulations. Multiple legal proceedings include California labor class actions, opioid litigation, and federal investigations regarding prescription medications. Self-insurance liabilities for employee health care and workers' compensation remain subject to claims volatility while commodity price fluctuations continue.
